title: panic when inspecting `docker://alexl/fedoratest:deltas`
body:
While reading through https://github.com/containers/image/pull/902, I wanted to have a look at the delta images and found Skopeo to panic:
```
~ $ skopeo inspect docker://alexl/fedoratest:deltas                          
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference      
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x40 pc=0x5642d5992d07]
...
```

`--raw` does not panic.

comments.body: Otoh, maybe we should generate some fake os/arch as these are nominally required fields.

Nice. I didn't read the full stack to not get side tracked. As you have the fix at hand, would you open a PR against c/image?

The image-spec states that the platform is optional but that it "SHOULD be present if its target is platform-specific." As delta images are specific to images of certain platforms, I we should set the platform there as well.

@vrothberg IT depends on your definition of "specific to". I mean you can apply deltas to any tarball, whether the result is target specific depends on the tarball itself. 

I guess we could copy the arch from the original layer info. Is that what you meant?
Yes, using the platform of the original image seems right.
This was brought in by #904 and released in Skopeo 1.0.0. Thanks again!
